title: Robust Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: A crowd-pleasing Malaysian dish featuring chicken marinated in a mixture of ground coriander and other spices, then grilled to perfection on a tava-griddle, served with a side of nutty and glassy-jelly peanut sauce.
instructions: Combine chicken breast, ground coriander, coconut milk, salt, black pepper, garlic, and ginger in a large bowl and mix well to coat the chicken.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to marinate.
Preheat a tava-griddle over medium-high heat and brush with a small amount of oil to prevent sticking.
Thread the marinated chicken strips onto skewers, if desired, and cook on the preheated tava-griddle for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Meanwhile, combine peanut butter, lime juice, and a pinch of salt in a small saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until smooth and glassy.
Serve the grilled chicken satay hot with the peanut sauce for dipping.
Garnish with chopped peanuts and fresh cilantro, if desired.
